digest.top_line: OptiTROP-Lung05: mPFS NR vs 5.7mo, HR 0.35; sac-TMT+pembro challenges pembro mono as 1L standard in PD-L1+ NSCLC.
digest.tldr: Thoracic dominated: OptiTROP-Lung05 (phase 3, HR 0.35) and ESAONA (phase 2, iORR 95.5% for asandeutertinib in 1L EGFR+/brain mets). Prostate highest volume: TALAPRO-3 extended PARP inhibition to HRR-def mHSPC, ARACOG confirmed darolutamide's cognitive edge vs enza, A-DREAM piloted ADT+ARPI interruption.

sites.intro: Two studies share the IO+CRT theme: RAD-IO hits its GO threshold (12-mo DFS 80%) while the session overview contextualizes pCR as a prognostic gate and positions ongoing bladder-sparing trials (EV209/EV309).

studies.name: RAD-IO
studies.tldr: 12-month DFS 80% (40/50; 95% CI 0.67-0.89) with durvalumab + chemoRT in MIBC, clearing GO threshold ≥75%; single-arm.
details: 📊 1° EP: 12-month DFS 80% (40/50; 95% CI 0.67-0.89); cleared pre-set GO threshold ≥75%
🔍 Multi-stage single-arm trial; durvalumab neoadjuvant, synchronous, and 12mo adjuvant with chemoRT (5FU+MMC, 55Gy/20Fr)
🔍 N=55 enrolled; N=54 received ≥1 treatment dose (1 withdrew pre-treatment); 33/54 (61%) completed planned treatment, 21/54 (39%) discontinued early
🔍 Node-positive subset: 55Gy/20Fr to bladder + 46Gy/20Fr to pelvic nodes
🔗 GO/NO-GO threshold derived from BC2001 historical CRT data; BC2001 CT vs RT DFS HR 0.78 (0.60-1.02), p=0.07 used as benchmark context
⚠️ Single-arm; no concurrent randomised comparator vs CRT alone; efficacy benchmarked against historical control only
⚠️ 39% early durvalumab discontinuation; adjuvant IO tolerability across 12mo is a key remaining question

figures.caption: Durvalumab completion: N=54; 33 (61%) completed planned treatment; 21 (39%) discontinued early.

verdict.rationale: Single-arm multi-stage feasibility trial; cleared GO threshold vs historical control only; randomised comparison vs CRT alone pending.
verdict.audience: MIBC cT2-T3, 13% node-positive, 75% prior neoadjuvant chemo, median age 69

open_questions: Randomised confirmation vs CRT alone required
Durability of bladder preservation beyond 12 months

studies.name: MIBC Management Post-pCR / Bladder-Sparing Session
studies.tldr: Durvalumab + CRT (55 Gy/20 fr) feasible in 54 MIBC (87% full RT); pCR after NAC carries 85% 5-yr OS per SWOG 8710.
details: 🔍 Durvalumab + CRT trial: N=54 MIBC, 55 Gy/20 fr + MMC/5-FU + durvalumab, single-arm phase 2
📊 RT delivery: 47/54 (87%) received full 55 Gy/20fr without extension or delay

subdetails: MMC reduced: 4 (7%); 5-FU Wk1 reduced: 9 (17%); 5-FU Wk4 administered: 42 (78%)

details: ⚠️ No efficacy endpoints reported; long-term bladder preservation, function, QOL, and safety require further follow-up
📊 pCR (pT0N0) as prognostic marker: SWOG 8710 85% 5-yr OS if pT0; meta-analysis pooled RR 0.19 for RFS
🔗 Perioperative standard shifting: NIAGARA 24-mo OS 82.2% vs 75.2% (Gem/Cis + perioperative durvalumab vs Gem/Cis alone)
🔗 VESPER: ddMVAC 5-yr OS 66% vs 57% over Gem/Cis; KEYNOTE-B15 EVP improved OS vs Gem/Cis (under FDA review)
🔗 Bladder-sparing EVP trials underway: EV209 (cystectomy-eligible, N=240, endpoints cCR + 2-yr BIEFS) and EV309 (ineligible/refusing, N=390, endpoints BIEFS + OS)

verdict.rationale: Delivery-only data, N=54 single-arm phase 2; no efficacy or bladder preservation endpoints reported yet.

open_questions: Long-term bladder preservation and QOL rates with durvalumab + CRT
Optimal post-pCR strategy: surveillance vs adjuvant IO
Will EVP bladder-sparing (EV209/EV309) improve on CRT outcomes?

sites.intro: Highest-volume site: TALAPRO-3 extends PARP inhibition to HRR-def mHSPC, A-DREAM pilots ADT+ARPI interruption, ARACOG settles the enza vs daro cognitive question, and NRG adds GC-score biomarker stratification for AAP intensification.

studies.name: NRG Clinico-Transcriptomic Risk Stratification (Abstract 5000)
studies.tldr: 22-gene GC independently predicts MFS, DM, and OS; combined NRG score reclassifies ~25% discordant NCCN high-risk pts for AAP intensification.
details: 📊 22-gene GC independently predicts MFS, DM, and OS beyond clinical variables alone (p < 0.001 for each endpoint)
📊 ~25% of NCCN ≥HR pts have discordant clinical vs. GC risk, warranting the combined approach

details.text: 🔍 Combined clinico-transcriptomic (CT) score: NCCN points + GC points
subdetails: NCCN HR = 1 pt; NCCN VHR = 2 pts
GC <HR = 0 pt; GC HR = 1 pt; GC VHR = 2 pts
CT HR (≤2 pts) → RT+ADT; CT VHR (≥3 pts) → RT+ADT+AAP

details: 🔗 STAMPEDE MO calibration anchor (Attard, Lancet 2022): HRMFS 0.53 (95% CI 0.44-0.64), HROS 0.60 (0.48-0.73), both p < 0.0001 for AAP intensification
⚠️ Framework developed on existing NRG trial data; no prospective RCT validating CT-score-guided treatment allocation reported
⚠️ Design not fully specified in source; likely secondary correlative analysis; GC cutoffs extend Spratt et al. (JCO 2018) prior work

verdict.rationale: Biomarker stratification framework on existing trial data; prospective RCT validating CT-score-guided AAP allocation not yet reported.
verdict.audience: NCCN ≥ high-risk localized prostate cancer, RT+ADT candidates

open_questions: Prospective RCT: does CT-score-guided allocation improve outcomes vs. clinical risk alone?
Generalizability to modern ARSI-backbone ADT regimens
GC threshold stability across contemporary cohorts

studies.name: A-DREAM (ALLIANCE mAPMS)
studies.tldr: ADT+ARPI interruption in mHSPC: 41% treatment-free with eugonadal T at 18mo (1° EP); 38.5% still off-tx at 26.9mo FU.
details: 📐 Primary EP: 80% CI 33.1-48.9%, one-sided p=0.0249; pre-specified 80% CI threshold (not 95%)
📊 Median time to eugonadal T recovery: 9.0mo (95% CI 8.4-12.4)
🔍 Single-arm phase II, N=78 enrolled 07/2022-03/2024; PSA<0.2 after ≥18-24mo ADT + ≥12mo ARPI
🔍 Re-initiation triggers: PSA ≥5 ng/mL, radiographic change (PCWG3), or PrCa-related sx
📊 Disposition at 26.9mo: 38.5% still off-tx; 37.2% resumed ADT+ARPI per protocol; 9.0% started non-protocol tx
📊 rPFS from interruption: 15/78 events, median NE (32.5-NE); 12-mo est 94.6% (89.6-99.9%)
📊 OS: 4/78 events, median NE; causes: prostate cancer, MDS, influenza, MI
⚠️ Single-arm; no randomized comparator vs continuous ADT+ARPI
⚠️ 64.9% low-volume CHAARTED, 84.6% White; high-volume and diverse pts underrepresented
🔗 Intermittent ADT established in earlier hormone-sensitive trials; A-DREAM is first prospective signal for ADT+ARPI interruption in mHSPC

verdict.rationale: Single-arm phase 2, no randomized comparator vs continuous ADT+ARPI; 80% CI threshold for primary endpoint.
verdict.audience: mHSPC achieving PSA<0.2 after ≥18-24mo ADT+ARPI, low-volume predominant

open_questions: Impact on long-term OS vs continuous ADT+ARPI: randomized trial needed
Optimal re-initiation criteria in high-volume vs low-volume disease
Biomarker predictors of durable treatment-free interval

studies.name: ENZAMET + Decipher Biomarker Analysis
studies.tldr: Decipher >0.85 identifies mHSPC pts with OS benefit from docetaxel intensification; IPTW interaction p=0.04, HR 0.75 (0.43-1.33).
details: 🔍 Biomarker analysis within ENZAMET phase 3 RCT; ADT+enza+doce cohort N=320 with evaluable Decipher; DPMC 0.85 cutoff per statistical analysis plan
⚠️ Individual arm HRs non-significant in IPTW-MVA (Decipher >0.85: HR 0.75, p=0.33; Decipher ≤0.85: HR 1.94, p=0.07); signal rests on interaction term only
⚠️ Tissue available in only ~427/3816 ENZAMET pts; representativeness of the analyzed subset uncertain
⚠️ ENZAMET predated current ARSI-doublet SOC; Decipher model trained pre-ARPI — applicability to modern ADT+ARSI backbone unvalidated
🔗 Framed as Level 1B evidence; consistent with CHARTED and STAMPEDE docetaxel predictive patterns

columns: OS HR triplet vs doublet (95% CI)

verdict.rationale: Post-hoc biomarker subset (N=320 of 3816); interaction-only signal; model trained pre-ARPI and not prospectively validated.
verdict.audience: mHSPC, ADT + enzalutamide backbone, tumor tissue available for Decipher testing

open_questions: Prospective Decipher-guided triplet selection trial in ARPI-era mHSPC needed
Optimal DPMC cutoff in pts receiving ADT+ARSI (not ENZA) doublets

studies.name: ARACOG (AFT-47)
studies.tldr: MCCD median -36.1 (ENZ) vs -15.8 (DAR) at 24wk, p=0.009; enzalutamide caused significantly greater cognitive decline.
details: 🔍 Randomized open-label phase 2, N=111 (mHSPC, nmCRPC, mCRPC), DAR vs ENZ; enrolled 8/2021-3/2025
📊 1° EP: CANTAB MCCD at 24wk median change -36.1 (ENZ, N=47) vs -15.8 (DAR, N=48), p=0.009
🔍 MCCD = maximally changed cognitive domain across 5 remote CANTAB tests: executive function, visual memory, attention, working memory
💊 Darolutamide provided by study; enzalutamide through standard of care
⚠️ Open-label; crossover allowed before 24wk, analyzed at crossover timepoint in randomized arm
🔗 Consistent with pharmacology: darolutamide has substantially lower CNS penetration vs enzalutamide, predicting less cognitive toxicity mechanistically
⚠️ CANTAB modules are research instruments, not clinical cognitive diagnostic tools

verdict.rationale: Randomized phase 2 hitting prespecified primary; confirms pharmacological expectation that lower CNS-penetrant darolutamide spares cognition vs enzalutamide.
verdict.audience: mHSPC, nmCRPC, or mCRPC on ARSI; cognitive toxicity a concern

open_questions: Cognitive difference durability beyond 24 weeks
Whether MCCD effect size translates to clinically meaningful QoL impact
Disease-state subgroup breakdown (mHSPC vs nmCRPC vs mCRPC)

studies.name: TALAPRO-3
studies.tldr: 3yr rPFS 77% vs 56% (HR 0.48, p<0.001) favoring talazoparib+enza+ADT in HRR-deficient mHSPC.
details: 🔍 Phase 3 RCT; N=599; HRR-deficient mHSPC; talazoparib+enza+ADT vs placebo+enza+ADT
🔗 vs TALAPRO-2 (talazoparib+enza, mCRPC, HRR+, HR ~0.46): class effect now established in hormone-sensitive setting
⚠️ Primary endpoint imaging-based rPFS, not OS; OS maturity not reported in source
⚠️ Non-BRCA HRR effect attenuated (HR 0.57 vs BRCA HR 0.37); non-BRCA HRR subset heterogeneous

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 RCT, primary endpoint hit (HR 0.48, p<0.001); establishes PARPi+ARSI as standard in HRR-deficient mHSPC.

open_questions: OS benefit magnitude and maturity
Which non-BRCA HRR alterations drive clinically meaningful rPFS benefit
Post-progression sequencing in PARP-pretreated pts transitioning to mCRPC

sites.intro: NSCLC carried a phase 3 ADC+IO vs IO-mono readout (OptiTROP-Lung05, HR 0.35) and the first randomized EGFR TKI head-to-head in brain-met-enriched 1L disease (ESAONA), plus maturing OS data for ami+laz in atypical EGFR+ (CHRYSALIS-2).

studies.name: CHRYSALIS-2 (1L Ami+Laz, Atypical EGFR+)
studies.tldr: Median OS 41.0 mo (95% CI 27.7-NE) with ami+laz in 1L atypical EGFR+ advanced NSCLC; single-arm, n=49.
details: 📊 Median OS 41.0 mo (95% CI 27.7-NE); median follow-up 31.3 mo
🔍 Single-arm, n=49; 1L atypical EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC
💊 Median treatment duration 13.3 mo (range <0.1-53.2)
💊 39% remained on treatment >2 years
🔗 Yang JCH NEJM 2025 (ami+laz 1L common EGFR): durable OS now reported in both common and atypical EGFR-mutated NSCLC populations
⚠️ Single-arm; no randomized comparator vs osimertinib or chemotherapy
⚠️ Small N (49) limits any subgroup analysis by EGFR variant subtype
⚠️ No clear EGFR variant subtype-outcome association found; underpowered to confirm or exclude

figures.caption: OS: median 41.0 mo (95% CI 27.7-NE); landmark rates 85%, 72%, 55%; median f/u 31.3 mo; n at risk 49 → 10 → 0.
Median treatment duration 13.3 mo (range <0.1-53.2); 39% on treatment >2 years.

verdict.rationale: Single-arm, n=49; no randomized comparator. Hard single-arm maturity gate applies regardless of follow-up duration.
verdict.audience: Advanced NSCLC, atypical EGFR mutation, treatment-naive

open_questions: Randomized trial vs osimertinib in atypical EGFR needed to confirm benefit
CNS penetration and activity across atypical EGFR variant subtypes
SC amivantamab formulation (COPERNICUS) applicability in this population

studies.name: ESAONA
studies.tldr: Intracranial ORR 95.5% vs 79.6% (p=0.0004), iPFS HR 0.46 favoring asandeutertinib over osimertinib in 1L EGFR+ NSCLC with brain mets.
details: 🔍 Phase II, randomized, N=224; 1L EGFR-mutated NSCLC with brain mets, asandeutertinib (n=111) vs osimertinib (n=113)
💊 Asandeutertinib: next-generation EGFR TKI evaluated head-to-head vs established osimertinib SOC

subdetails: Serious TRAEs: 10.8% vs 7.1% — slightly higher in experimental arm

details: 🔗 Osimertinib established 1L SOC via FLAURA; ESAONA is first randomized head-to-head with a next-gen EGFR TKI in the brain-met-enriched setting
⚠️ Phase 2 only, N=224; PFS medians not reached in experimental arm (immature); OS data not reported in source
⚠️ Primary EP was iORR (response endpoint), not PFS or OS; longer follow-up required for survival readout

verdict.rationale: Randomized phase 2 hitting pre-specified 1° EP (iORR) vs osimertinib with iPFS HR 0.46; internally valid design for a divergent result against established SOC.
verdict.audience: 1L EGFR-mutated NSCLC with active brain metastases

open_questions: Phase 3 OS confirmatory data needed before practice adoption
Activity after progression on prior osimertinib unknown
Optimal sequencing vs osimertinib + chemo (FLAURA2) in brain-met population

studies.name: OptiTROP-Lung05
studies.tldr: mPFS NR vs 5.7mo, HR 0.35 (0.26-0.47), p<0.0001 favoring sac-TMT + pembro in 1L PD-L1+ NSCLC.
details: 📊 Descriptive OS (immature): HR 0.55 (95%CI 0.36-0.85); events 33 (15.9%) vs 54 (26.3%); median f/u 10.5mo
📊 PFS benefit consistent across both PD-L1 TPS subgroups (≥50% and 1-49%)
🔍 Phase 3 open-label RCT; N=413; stage IIIB/IIIC/IV NSCLC; PD-L1 TPS ≥1%; no EGFR/ALK; ECOG 0-1
💊 Sac-TMT 4mg/kg Q2W + pembro 400mg Q6W vs pembro 400mg Q6W; max 18 pembro cycles
🔗 Converges with TROPION-Lung08 (Dato-DXd+durvalumab vs pembro, TPS≥50%): ADC+IO > IO-mono signal emerging in 1L NSCLC
⚠️ OS immature at primary PFS analysis (10.5mo median f/u); definitive OS benefit not yet established
⚠️ Control is pembro mono; for TPS 1-49%, pembro+platinum chemo is also SOC — no head-to-head vs chemo-IO doublet

figures.caption: Descriptive OS (immature): HR 0.55 (95%CI 0.36-0.85). Events 33 (15.9%) vs 54 (26.3%). Median f/u 10.5 months.

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 RCT hitting primary PFS endpoint; OS immature at 10.5mo f/u prevents full practice-change classification. Consistent with emerging ADC+IO signal.
verdict.audience: Stage IIIB-IV NSCLC, PD-L1 TPS ≥1%, no EGFR/ALK, treatment-naive, ECOG 0-1

open_questions: Will PFS benefit translate to OS with longer follow-up?
Superiority vs. pembro + platinum chemo for TPS 1-49%?
Predictive biomarker beyond PD-L1 TPS for TROP2 ADC benefit?

sites.intro: PREPEC is the first RCT to quantify the PRO advantage of pre-pectoral IBBR; the BREAST-Q gain is statistically significant but modest, offset by higher implant loss.

studies.name: PREPEC
studies.tldr: BREAST-Q chest score 79.2 vs 74.3 (diff 4.8, p=0.01) favoring pre-pectoral IBBR; implant loss 21% vs 15%.
details: 🔍 International RCT; nipple-sparing or skin-sparing mastectomy; therapeutic + risk-reduction setting
🔍 1° EP: BREAST-Q physical well-being (chest) at 24 months; LS mean from linear mixed model; longitudinal completion 83-95%
⚠️ Main secondary safety EP missed non-inferiority: pre-pectoral had higher unplanned implant loss/replacement
⚠️ 4.8-point BREAST-Q difference is modest; minimum clinically important difference for this subscale is debated
🔗 Prior observational series suggested PRO advantage with pre-pectoral IBBR; PREPEC is first large RCT to test this directly

verdict.rationale: Phase II RCT; prespecified PRO primary hit; consistent with prior observational pre-pectoral PRO signals. Implant loss trade-off doesn't negate PRO verdict.
verdict.audience: Nipple-sparing or skin-sparing mastectomy for breast cancer treatment or risk re

open_questions: Does PRO benefit persist beyond 24 months?
Which pts tolerate higher implant failure risk of pre-pectoral?
Phase III confirmation warranted before routine adoption?

sites.intro: Neo-CRAG adds CRT to peri-op chemotherapy in high-risk LAGC, halving locoregional recurrence and extending OS; XELOX backbone limits direct FLOT-era applicability.

studies.name: Neo-CRAG
studies.tldr: Adding neoadj CRT to peri-op XELOX: mDFS 52.7 vs 24.4 mo, mOS 67.5 vs 37.6 mo in high-risk LAGC.
details: 📊 Locoregional recurrence in R0-resected pts: 9.4% CRT vs 18.3% CT
🔍 N=620, 13 Chinese centers, 2013-2022; cT3N2+ or T4 gastric/GEJ; 36.3% EGJ primary
🔍 CRT arm: 45 Gy/25 fractions concurrent after C1 chemo, with dose-reduced oxaliplatin (100 mg/m²) and capecitabine (825 mg/m²)
⚠️ Backbone is XELOX, not FLOT; limits direct applicability to FLOT-era Western practice
🔗 LRR halved despite D2 resection: supports a genuine RT contribution beyond chemo-alone locoregional control

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 RCT hitting DFS and OS; XELOX backbone limits direct translation to FLOT-era Western practice.
verdict.audience: Locally advanced gastric/GEJ adenocarcinoma (cT3N2+, T4), peri-op XELOX eligible

open_questions: Does adding RT to FLOT peri-op chemo confer similar DFS/OS benefit?
Optimal RT dose-fractionation in FLOT-era perioperative settings
Long-term LRR and OS durability beyond 5 years

sites.intro: ROADS is the first randomized comparison of GammaTile vs post-op SRS in resected brain mets >2cm; striking cavity control benefit, but higher LMD rate with brachytherapy warrants close attention.

studies.name: ROADS
studies.tldr: Surg bed recurrence 1% (GT) vs 12% (SRS); 2yr OS 62% vs 36% favoring GammaTile in resected brain mets >2cm.
details: 🔍 Randomized, N=230, resected brain mets >2cm; GammaTile brachytherapy vs post-op SRS
📊 Surg bed recurrence 1% GammaTile vs 12% SRS — primary EP
⚠️ LMD 10% GammaTile vs 3% SRS — leptomeningeal spread higher with brachytherapy
📊 Radiation necrosis similar: 8% GammaTile vs 7% SRS
🔗 vs Alliance N107C: established SRS as post-resection standard; ROADS directly randomizes vs GammaTile for the first time
⚠️ OS 62% vs 36% is a secondary endpoint; trial powered for cavity control, not OS confirmation
⚠️ Trial phase not stated in source; primary EPs both NR suggests follow-up still maturing for OS

verdict.rationale: First RCT showing GammaTile superiority over post-op SRS; both primary EPs hit with dramatic OS benefit in applicable population.

open_questions: Does LMD increase with GammaTile translate to OS detriment at longer follow-up?
Benefit extend to resected mets <2cm?
Optimal GammaTile activity prescription for cavity control

sites.intro: SPIN score offers pre-treatment patient selection for celiac plexus SRS, though the SPIN-2 subgroup (N=19) is too small for confident clinical adoption without external validation.

studies.name: SPIN Score (Celiac Plexus SRS)
studies.tldr: 89% pain response in SPIN-2 pts (baseline pain >6 + no prior neurotoxic chemo) vs 32% SPIN-0 after celiac SRS.
details: 📊 Pain response by SPIN score
🔍 Post-hoc analysis of 90 evaluable pts from pivotal ph2 (NCT03323489; Lancet Oncol 2024:25:1070-79)
📊 Pivotal ph2 overall response: 53% (95% CI 42-64%)
📐 Multivariate predictors: no prior neurotoxic chemo (OR 5.1, p=0.009); baseline pain >6 (OR 1.8, p=0.003)
📐 Optimism-corrected AUC 0.714 (bootstrap 500 iterations)
📐 Pain threshold dose-response: >6 optimal (65.8%); >7 (83.3%); >8 (85.7%)
⚠️ Post-hoc design; internal validation only; external validation required before clinical use
⚠️ SPIN-2 subgroup N=19; response estimate imprecise

verdict.rationale: Post-hoc analysis of ph2; N=90; SPIN-2 subgroup N=19. Internal validation only; external validation explicitly pending.
verdict.audience: Pancreatic cancer, intractable retroperitoneal pain, planned celiac plexus SRS

open_questions: External validation before clinical implementation
Applicability to non-pancreatic retroperitoneal malignancies
How early before neurotoxic chemo should SRS be offered?

Lay Summary
• In women having surgery to remove the breast for cancer treatment or prevention, the
optimal positioning of an implant (above the chest muscle, or below it) to rebuild the breast is
currently unclear.
• In the international PREPEC trial that was designed together with patient advocates, women
were assigned by chance (i.e., randomized) to receive the implant above or below the
muscle.
• The primary endpoint of the study was long-term patient-reported physical well-being of the
chest.
• Rebuilding the breast with an implant above the chest muscle resulted in better physical
well-being of the chest after two years (difference of 4.8 points on the BREAST-Q scale).
• However, implants placed above the chest muscle were more likely to undergo unexpected
removal or replacement (21% versus 15% of patients).

Perioperative MIBC: An Evolving Standard
Standard of care is defined by cisplatin-eligibility - with immunotherapy-based "sandwich" regimens
reshaping practice.
Cisplatin-ineligible or declining
• KEYNOTE-905: perioperative enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab (EVP) is now standard
• 3 cycles EVP → cystectomy → 6 cycles EV + 14 cycles P:
• NEW: VOLGA: preoperative EV + perioperative durvalumab + tremelimumab
• EV + durva improved EFS & OS vs cystectomy * approved adjuvant therapy; EV + durva/treme improved EFS (press release)?
Cisplatin-eligible
• NIAGARA: pre-op Gem/Cis + perioperative durvalumab (24-mo OS 82.2% vs 75.2% with Gem/Cis alone)
• VESPER: established ddMVAC > Gem/Cis on survival (5-yr OS 66% vs 57%) but greater toxicity*
• NEW: KEYNOTE-B15: perioperative EVP improved OS vs Gem/Cis (under FDA review)

Management Post-pCR: Continue or Stop?
For the sandwich regimens, standard practice continues planned
adjuvant therapy regardless of pathologic response.
Standard practice today
• NIAGARA regimen: Resume durvalumab for another 8 months post-cystectomy:
Perioperative EVP: Resume EVP post-cystectomy
Notably, not all patients in perioperative trials completed adjuvant therapy, sometimes due to toxicity
Cisplatin-based chemo alone: Surveillance after pCR is standard
Unanswered questions
What is the relative contribution of the pre- vs postoperative components?
Can adjuvant therapy be safely de-escalated based on pCR or other biomarkers?

Conclusions
• This single-arm study of amivantamab + lazertinib (n=49) in 1L atypical EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC
demonstrates a clinically meaningful median OS of ~3.5 years
• Responses were durable regardless of demographics, baseline tumor mutations, and disease characteristic:

with no new safety signals

in both common and atypical EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC

image_ocr_texts:
The SPIN Score, a simple clinical score to predict pain response following celiac plexus
Abstract # 546246
Board # 194
radiosurgery for retroperitoneal cancer pain in pancreatic cancer.
Yaacov Lawrence Lawrence, Marcin Miszczyk, Dayssy Alexandra Diaz Pardo, Artur Aguiar, Dror Limon, M. Raphael Pfeffer, Michael Buckstein, Aisling S Barry, Adam P. Dicker, Camilla Zimmermann,
Jordan Kharofa, Ofer Margalit, Orly Yariv, Ofir Morag, David Hausner, Maoz Ben-Ayun, Talia Golan, Laura A. Dawson, Zvi Symon
SHEBA
Tel HaShomer
The Benjamin (Beni) Davidai
Department of
Radiation Oncology
Background
Celiac plexus radiosurgery is a novel non-invasive palliative treatment that was
recently added to NCCN guidelines. In the pivotal phase 2 trial (NCT03323489;
Lancet Oncol 2024;25:1070-79), pain response was 53% (95% Cl 42-64%).
Prespecified exploratory analysis identified age and BMI as predictors; however, no
clinically actionable patient selection tool was developed. We sought to identify
additional predictors and create a practical risk stratification score.
Methods
Post-hoc analysis of 90 evaluable patients from the phase 2 trial. Pain response was
defined per protocol (22-point reduction in average pain from baseline to three
weeks, on Brief Pain Inventory-Short Form). Candidate baseline predictors were
examined by univariate and multivariate logistic regression. Only variables
remaining significant in multivariate analysis were included in the final score to
ensure independence and avoid overfitting. Internal validation used bootstrap
resampling (500 iterations with replacement) to calculate optimism-corrected AUC.
Analysis performed using Stata IC/16.1.
Results
Univariate analysis identified 4 significant predictors: neurotoxic chemotherapy
exposure (OR 5.33, 95% Cl 2.13-13.4, p<0.001), baseline pain intensity (OR 1.73,
p=0.003), age (OR 1.06, p=0.014), and therapy line (OR 0.65, p=0.04). On
multivariate analysis, only neurotoxic exposure (OR 5.1, p=0.009) and baseline pain
(OR 1.8, p=0.003) remained independently significant predictors. Age and therapy
line lost significance due to collinearity.
Dose-response analysis
confirmed
pain threshold
optimization: >5
(61.5%
response), >6 (65.8%), >7 (83.3%), >8 (85.7%), with >6 providing optimal
discrimination.
We created the SPIN (Severe Pain + Intact Nerves) score (0-2
points): baseline Pain >6 (+1), No prior Neurotoxic chemotherapy (+1). Response
rates by score: 0 points: 32% (n=31); 1 point: 53% (n=40); 2 points: 89% (n=19). The
2-variable model achieved an apparent AUC=0.716. Following bootstrapping, the
optimism-corrected AUC was= 0.714
Conclusions
• The simple score allows identification of distinct patient subgroups with markedly different probabilities of pain response
following celiac plexus radiosurgery.
• This suggests the intervention should be considered earlier in the disease course, before exposure to neurotoxic agents.
External validation is needed prior to clinical implementation.

our previous studies
• Very high bladder preservation rates
• Very high overall and disease-free survival versus previous trial data
